finals.title: TECH & SCIENCE FINAL
finals.clue: Wilhelm Conrad Röntgen is credited with the discovery of this form of electromagnetic radiation, now used ubiquitously in medical imaging.

finals.title: COMPUTING SECURITY
finals.clue: Discovered in 2014, this critical security flaw in the Unix Bash shell allowed attackers to execute arbitrary commands, and was named evocatively for its destructive potential.
finals.fit_justification: Source category is literally 'Science: Computers'; source question asks 'What was the name of the security vulnerability found in Bash in 2014?' and source answer is 'Shellshock' — a specific, niche piece of computing security history appropriate for a Final.

finals.title: TECHNOLOGY PIONEER
finals.clue: Apple was founded in California, but this rival technology giant — whose products include Windows — was founded in a western state known as the Land of Enchantment.
finals.fit_justification: Source is categorized 'Science: Computers'; question asks 'in which western state was Microsoft founded?' naming both Apple and Microsoft; answer 'New Mexico' is directly stated in source.

finals.title: NUCLEAR INCIDENT
finals.clue: In March 1979, a partial meltdown of a reactor core caused the most significant nuclear accident in U.S. history, occurring at this facility in Pennsylvania.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'In March 1979, where did a major nuclear accident occur?' — about a nuclear technology disaster; source answer is 'Three Mile Island'.

finals.title: COMPUTING FUNDAMENTALS
finals.clue: A foundational unit of digital data, this quantum of computer memory contains exactly eight of a smaller unit — and every byte you've ever processed is made of them.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'How many bits are commonly in a single byte?' and is explicitly categorized as 'Science: Computers'; source answer 'Eight bits' is the canonical computing fact about byte composition.

finals.title: TECHNOLOGY MILESTONE
finals.clue: Invented in 1980, this optical disc format revolutionized how music and data were stored and distributed, replacing vinyl and magnetic tape for many consumers.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'In which year was the Compact Disc invented?' with answer '1980' — the Compact Disc is a landmark technology invention explicitly named in the source question.
